title: sambucus nigra f. alba
intro: Sambucus nigra f. alba, also known as White Elder or Elderflower, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Sambucus genus and is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Sambucus nigra f. alba include reducing symptoms of the common cold and flu, alleviating allergies and sinus congestion, and providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Sambucus nigra f. alba include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a supplement or used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
